justification: Output is valid JSON with no markdown fences. business_rules array has 8 entries (≥5), each with id and rule fields. acceptance_criteria array has 7 entries (≥5), each with given/when/then fields. edge_cases array has 5 entries (≥2). confidence object has numeric level (95). Rules are domain-specific to passbar rendering, including null handling (BR-4) and zero-total behavior (BR-2).

observations.text: Clean JSON output with no markdown fences
8 business rules with invariant flags and testable assertions — exceeds minimum
7 acceptance criteria covering mixed, all-fail, all-pass, all-zero, and null cases
5 edge cases including negative counts and very small segments
BR-4 specifically addresses null handling, BR-2 addresses zero-total — both required by rubric
BR-8 captures the flex:1 rounding detail showing deep understanding of the rendering domain
Includes business_impact and escalations sections beyond what was required

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"The eval dashboard displays evaluation run results for a DreamTeam AI agent system. Each run row includes a horizontal progress bar (passbar) showing how many scenarios passed, partially passed, or failed. This is a data visualization component that must faithfully represent the underlying evaluation data to support decision-making about agent quality.","bounded_context":"Eval Dashboard — web UI for browsing and comparing agent evaluation runs","ubiquitous
